revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:二子塚古墳 (池田市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Futagozuka Kofun (二子塚古墳) | image =
Mound | alternate_name = Inariyama Kofun / Shinke Inariyama Kofun{{Sfn|New History of Ikeda City, Vol. 1|1997}} | location = {{ill|Ikeda|qid=Q817965|1=Ikeda, Osaka}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.813167|N|135.444153|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-27 | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (Keyhole-shaped tomb) | area = Mound length: 45m | burial = Rear circular part: Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoanashiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanashiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
Front rectangular part: Yokoanashiki sekishitsu | burial goods = {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}{{Sfn|Futagozuka Kofun (Ikeda)|1989}} | built = Early 6th century | Interred_individuals = | Historic Site = None | notes = | notes = | map = Japan Osaka | type = Zenpokoenfun-NE | map_dot_label = Futagozuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top | image = Futagozuka_Kofun_%28Ikeda%29%2C_funkyu.jpg | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Futagozuka Kofun''' (二子塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Ikeda, Osaka|en|Ikeda, Osaka|qid=Q817965|lt=Ikeda, Osaka|1=Ikeda, Osaka}}, Osaka Prefecture. The kofun is not Historic Site1 as a historic site. == Overview == This kofun is situated at the southern end of a hill extending south from the Hatano Plateau in northern Osaka Prefecture. Presently, an Inari shrine is enshrined on the mound. The kofun was first introduced to academia in 1914 (Taisho 3) by {{ill|Sueji Umehara|ja|梅原末治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueji Umehara|qq=}}, and excavation surveys (the first phase) were conducted in 1986 (Showa 61).{{Sfn|Ikeda City Excavation Report 1986|1987}} The mound has a keyhole-shaped design, typeerly considered a pair of circular mounds.{{Sfn|Ikeda City Excavation Report 1986|1987}} The front rectangular part faces northeast, and the total length is approximately 45 meters. No traces of [[Fukiishi]] or {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} have been found.Historical site explanation board. The burial facilities consist of two yokoanashiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chambers), one each in the rear circular part and the front rectangular part. The rear circular chamber (south chamber) is double-sleeved and measures 6.7 meters in total length, but the chamber in the front rectangular part (north chamber) remains buried and its details are unknown. The associated burial goods are also not fully identified.{{Sfn|Ikeda City Excavation Report 1986|1987}} The construction date is estimated to be in the early 6th century, during the early Kofun period, or possibly the mid-6th century.{{Sfn|New History of Ikeda City, Vol. 1|1997}} It is considered the last zenpokoenfun in the Ina River basin. Futagozuka Kofun (Ikeda), genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (toward the rear wall) Futagozuka Kofun (Ikeda), genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (toward the entrance) Futagozuka Kofun (Ikeda), sendou.jpg|Passage (toward the main chamber) Futagozuka Kofun (Ikeda), Inari-jinja.jpg|Inari shrine atop the mound 二子塚古墳 (池田市) 出土 須恵器片.JPG|Sue pottery fragments
{{small|Displayed at {{ill|Ikeda City Historical Folklore Museum|ja|池田市立歴史民俗資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda City Historical Folklore Museum|qq=}}.}} == notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Explanation board by Ikeda City Board of Education, 2011. * Local government publications: ''' {{Cite book|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/5256 Ikeda City Excavation Report 1986 (Cultural Property Report No. 5)]|publisher=Ikeda City Board of Education|year=1987|ref={{Harvid|Ikeda City Excavation Report 1986|1987}}}} (link via Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site' "Nationwide Site Reports Database"). ''' {{Cite book|title=New History of Ikeda City, Vol. 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient and Medieval Periods)|publisher=Ikeda City|year=1997|ref={{Harvid|New History of Ikeda City, Vol. 1|1997}}}} * Encyclopedic works: ''' {{Cite book|title=[[Dictionary of Infobox ancient site]]|author=Takashima Toru|publisher=[[Tōkyōdō Shuppan]]|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Futagozuka Kofun (Ikeda)|1989}}}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Futagozuka Kofun (Ikeda)}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] [[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]] [[Template:infobox ancient site]] [[File:Futagozuka Kofun (Ikeda), funkyu.jpg|280px]] {{translated page|ja|二子塚古墳 (池田市)|version=104109905|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:二子塚古墳 (池田市)]] [[ja:二子塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Futagozuka Kofun (Ikeda)}} {{wikidata link|Q80363004}} [[Category:History of Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
